steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: The volume of a right circular cylinder is $V = \pi r^2 h$. The first cylinder has volume $V_1 = \pi r^2 h$. The second cylinder must have volume $V_2 = 392 \cdot \pi r^2 h$, so $\pi R^2 H = 392 \pi r^2 h$, which simplifies to $R^2 H = 392 r^2 h$.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: Factor 392: $392 = 8 \times 49 = 8 \times 7^2$. So the ratio must satisfy $\frac{R^2}{r^2} \cdot \frac{H}{h} = 392$.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: Test each listed pair by computing $\frac{R^2}{r^2} \cdot \frac{H}{h}$: $R=8r$ and $H=7h$ gives $64 \times 7 = 448$; $R=8r$ and $H=49h$ gives $64 \times 49 = 3{,}136$; $R=7r$ and $H=8h$ gives $49 \times 8 = 392$; $R=49r$ and $H=8h$ gives $2{,}401 \times 8 = 19{,}208$.
